location.office_time_required: 3 days in the office per week

secondary_archetype: Program Manager with safety/integrity domain focus

level_signals: Compensation range $162K-$240K USD consistent with senior IC at OpenAI
Extensive track record of delivering high-profile, complex projects
Directly partners with engineering and fundamental research teams of the highest caliber
Graduate degree or equivalent experience required

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Graduate degree in computer science, engineering, management or equivalent experience.
Have an extensive track record of successfully delivering high-profile, complex projects against tight deadlines.
Are technically versed, and have effectively partnered with engineering and fundamental research teams of the highest caliber.
Have expertise in designing and implementing simple, scalable processes that solve complex problems.
Are relentlessly resourceful and thrive in ambiguous, fast-paced environments.
Have strong knowledge of content integrity and moderation, including industry best practices and regulatory guidelines.
Have exceptional written and verbal communication skills.

scope.surface: Safety Systems — safety review processes, model and product launch readiness, risk tables, incident management, safeguards tooling
primary_metrics_inferred: Safety review completion rate across model/product launches
Risk table coverage and mitigation resolution rate
Scalability and repeatability of safety processes
core_responsibilities: Coordinate cross-functional safety launch readiness across model and product releases, ensuring risk tables, mitigations, monitoring plans, and decision points are clearly tracked from early scoping through post-launch review.

Build operating rhythms across Safety Systems, Integrity, Strategy, User Operations, and engineering teams so safety learnings from past launches translate into concrete improvements to automated safeguards, tooling, and review processes.
Design and run key internal programs, including incident management and processes for regularly updating our safety mitigations.
Work directly with technical stakeholders to create scalable, repeatable safety processes.

green_flags: Clear mission-driven role with well-defined responsibilities
Relocation assistance offered to new employees
Role has concrete, well-scoped ownership (safety review process, launch readiness, incident management)
Cross-functional exposure across research, engineering, legal, policy, and product
red_flags: No visa sponsorship information explicitly stated — unclear for international candidates
Onsite requirement is 3 days/week in San Francisco — eliminates remote candidates
No equity or benefits details disclosed beyond base salary range
Graduate degree listed under 'you might thrive if' framing but is a substantive credential expectation — classification boundary is ambiguous

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from compensation range and seniority signals; not explicitly stated)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.work_auth (inferred as open from equal opportunity statement; not explicitly addressed for work auth)
location.visa_sponsorship (not mentioned in JD; marked unclear)
ambiguities: All qualifications are framed under 'You might thrive in this role if you:' which softens the language, but the items read as substantive requirements rather than preferences. Classified as must_haves (hard) given the unconditional, non-'preferred' framing and the operational criticality of the role.
Graduate degree requirement uses 'or equivalent experience' — treated as hard credential with accepts_equivalent_experience: true.
Equity not mentioned anywhere in the JD — equity_mentioned set to false, though it is standard at OpenAI.
